offer-clarity.question: how do I know if my offer will actually sell?
offer-clarity.answerHeading: Test it before you build it.
answerBody: Most coaches build the whole thing first, then hope. Reverse it. Write the offer as one clear page — the outcome, the roadmap, the price — and put it in front of real buyers before you create anything.
If a stranger reads it and buys without a call, it's validated. If they have ten questions, it's not clear enough yet — and now you know exactly what to fix.
For a $27 to $5K path, the answer was always obvious: validate first, build second.

no-calls.question: how do I get high-ticket clients without sales calls?
answerBody: Instead of a sales call, build one clear offer a qualified prospect reads, sees the price and the proof, and decides on — no Zoom, no DMs, no follow-up.
The reason you need a call is usually that the offer is too complex. Make it clear enough to stand on its own and the call becomes optional.
Clarity does the selling. Not you.

grow-into-pain.headline: You can't *grow into pain.*

one-afternoon.headline: One clear offer. *One afternoon.*
one-afternoon.subline: AI does the heavy lifting. You decide.

why-not-selling.title: I figured out why my offer wasn't selling — and it wasn't the price
body: For a year I kept lowering my price and adding bonuses. Still crickets.
Turns out the problem was clarity, not cost. My offer needed a 40-minute call just to explain. If it needs explaining, it's too vague to buy.
I rebuilt it as one clear page in an afternoon — outcome, roadmap, price. First person who read it just bought. No call.
Wish someone had told me: validate the clarity before you build the thing.

the-shift.title: Same skills. Different offer.

lines: “Let me hop on a call and walk you through how this works…”
Rewriting the offer for every lead.

the-shift.footer: The price was never the problem. The offer was.

5-signs.title: 5 Signs Your Offer Won't Sell
5-signs.subtitle: And why lowering the price won't fix it
items: It takes you more than a sentence to explain it.
Prospects have ten questions before they decide.
You rewrite it for every single lead.
You need a call to “walk them through it.”
5-signs.closer: All five are a *clarity* problem — not a price problem.
